title: Whole Blood in Trauma Patients With Hemorrhagic Shock

skipped_criteria: More than 4 hours from trauma to hospital admission: requires temporal/time-from-injury field not in schema.
More than 2 hours from hospital admission to randomization: requires time-from-admission field not in schema.
Transfusion of more than one packed red blood cell unit prior to randomization: requires prior transfusion count field not in schema.
Patients who have undergone surgery (laparotomy, thoracotomy, or sternotomy) before hospital admission: requires prior surgical history field not in schema.
In-extremis patients with devastating injuries (expected to die within 60 minutes): requires clinical prognosis/expected survival field not in schema.
Blood group other than O or A and positive Rh factor: requires blood type/Rh factor field not in schema.

Severe traumatic brain injury in which neurosurgical intervention is futile (partial decapitation, massive intracranial hemorrhage, or transcranial gunshot wounds): requires nuanced TBI severity/futility assessment beyond available boolean fields.
Burns over 20% of the total body surface area: requires burn TBSA percentage field not in schema.
Suspected airway burn: requires airway burn field not in schema.
CPR for more than 5 minutes before randomization: requires CPR duration field not in schema; mechanism=cardiac_arrest used as a proxy for pre-arrival CPR exclusion only.
Do not resuscitate order: requires DNR/advance directive field not in schema.
Incarcerated/prisoners: requires legal/custody status field not in schema.
Patient or legal representative who refuse to participate: requires consent status field not in schema.
Candidate for massive transfusion (ABC Score >= 2 or physician discretion): ABC score is a composite clinical score not directly representable in schema fields.
Concurrent availability of whole blood or blood component therapy: requires resource/inventory availability field not in schema.
Trauma activation level rule used as partial proxy for hemorrhagic shock trauma code activation, but the schema does not distinguish hemorrhagic shock specifically.
